en: Dead flies make the perfumer's ointment give off a stench; so a little folly outweighs wisdom and honor.
zh: 幾隻死蒼蠅能使做香料者的膏油發霉變臭，一點點愚昧比智慧和榮譽的影響更大。

en: A wise man's heart inclines him to the right, but a fool's heart to the left.
zh: 智慧人的心向右，愚昧人的心偏左。

en: Even when the fool walks on the road, he lacks sense, and he says to everyone that he is a fool.
zh: 愚昧人連走路的時候，也顯出無知；他對每一個人表現出他是個愚昧人 。

en: If the anger of the ruler rises against you, do not leave your place, for calmness will lay great offenses to rest.
zh: 如果掌權者向你發怒，你不要離開原位，因為柔順能免大過。

en: There is an evil that I have seen under the sun, as it were an error proceeding from the ruler:
zh: 在日光之下我看見一件憾事，好像是出於官長無意的錯誤，

en: folly is set in many high places, and the rich sit in a low place.
zh: 就是愚昧人得居眾多高位，富有的人卻處卑位。

en: I have seen slaves on horses, and princes walking on the ground like slaves.
zh: 我見過奴僕騎馬，貴族卻像奴僕一樣在地上步行。

en: He who digs a pit will fall into it, and a serpent will bite him who breaks through a wall.
zh: 挖陷阱的，自己掉進去；拆牆垣的，有蛇來咬他。

en: He who quarries stones is hurt by them, and he who splits logs is endangered by them.
zh: 採石的，被石打傷；劈柴的，被柴損害。

en: If the iron is blunt, and one does not sharpen the edge, he must use more strength, but wisdom helps one to succeed.
zh: 斧頭若鈍了，還不把斧刃磨快，就必多費力氣；智慧的好處在於助人成功。

en: If the serpent bites before it is charmed, there is no advantage to the charmer.
zh: 未行法術先被蛇咬，那麼，法術對行法術的人就毫無用處。

en: The words of a wise man's mouth win him favor, but the lips of a fool consume him.
zh: 智慧人的口，說出恩言；愚昧人的嘴，卻吞滅自己。

en: The beginning of the words of his mouth is foolishness, and the end of his talk is evil madness.
zh: 愚昧人的話開頭是愚昧，結尾是邪惡狂妄。

en: A fool multiplies words, though no man knows what is to be, and who can tell him what will be after him?
zh: 愚昧人多言多語。人不知道將來會有甚麼事，誰能告訴他死後會發生甚麼事呢？

en: The toil of a fool wearies him, for he does not know the way to the city.
zh: 愚昧人的勞碌徒使自己困乏，他連怎樣進城也不知道。

en: Woe to you, O land, when your king is a child, and your princes feast in the morning!
zh: 邦國啊，如果你的王是個孩童，而你的大臣又一早吃喝宴樂，你就有禍了。

en: Happy are you, O land, when your king is the son of the nobility, and your princes feast at the proper time, for strength, and not for drunkenness!
zh: 邦國啊，如果你的王是貴冑之子，而你的大臣又按時吃喝，為要得力，不為醉酒，你就有福了。

en: Through sloth the roof sinks in, and through indolence the house leaks.
zh: 房頂塌下是因為懶惰，房屋滴漏是因為懶得動手。

en: Bread is made for laughter, and wine gladdens life, and money answers everything.
zh: 設宴是為歡笑，酒能使人生快活，金錢能解決萬事。

en: Even in your thought, do not curse the king, nor in your bedroom curse the rich, for a bird of the air will carry your voice, or some winged creature tell the matter.
zh: 不要在意念中咒罵君王，也不要在臥房中咒罵財主，因為空中的飛鳥會傳聲，有翅膀的會述說這事。
